extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ won the 8.2km time trial by 19 seconds over YATES, taking 23 seconds on NIBALI through devastating climbing speed in the final 2km.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ paced first 6km conservatively, losing 18s to YATES on flat, then destroyed field on 2km climb at 10%+.
NIBALI beat pure time trialists DUMOULIN and LOPEZ despite inferior flat speed, signaling exceptional climbing form early.
YATES rode evenly-paced effort, staying within 18s on flat but unable to match ROGLIČ's explosive climb acceleration.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: 23-second margin over specialist climbers on 2km steep finish suggests ability to take time on mountain stages, not just defend
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Second place 19s down despite losing 18s on flat section indicates climbing legs match expectations from 2018
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: Third at 23s, beating time trial specialists DUMOULIN and LOPEZ on mixed terrain reveals sharp early-season condition
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: Fifth place tied with LÓPEZ at 28s, losing to NIBALI on climbing finish suggests suboptimal preparation
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: neutral: Described as impressing with two stage wins at Tour of Alps, first pro victories before Giro
